{{Quote|My speeder might be built like a dung beetle, but it could survive an acid storm on Mordis VI.|[[Zam Wesell/Legends|Zam Wesell]]|<ref name="The New Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels">''[[The New Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]''</ref>}}
The '''''Koro-2'' all-environment exodrive airspeeder''', also known as the '''''Koro-2'' exodrive airspeeder''', was a model of dragster-style [[Airspeeder/Legends|airspeeder]] that was manufactured and sold by the [[Desler Gizh Outworld Mobility Corporation/Legends|Desler Gizh Outworld Mobility Corporation]]. The [[Clawdite/Legends|Clawdite]] [[Assassin/Legends|assassin]] [[Zam Wesell/Legends|Zam Wesell]] favored this type of speeder, using one of them during her work prior to the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. The ''Koro-2'' was an upgraded version of the original [[Koro-1 airspeeder|''Koro-1'' speeder]].

==History==
[[File:Koro-2.jpg|thumb|left|200px|[[Zam Wesell/Legends|Zam Wesell's]] ''Koro''-2 on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]]]

Zam Wesell's ''Koro-2'' was the assassin's personal model, used as a lean, speedy getaway vehicle. Designed to survive in hostile environments, the pressurized cockpit had a two-week air supply. Zam's speeder featured no offensive weapons, relying strictly on speed and agility—or, on occasion, Zam's [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]], fired out the window at fleeing targets or pursuing law enforcers. Acquired on one of the billions of heavily metallic worlds controlled by the [[Mining Guild/Legends|Mining Guild]], Zam went against the designer's wishes to keep the vehicle on hostile planets by introducing it to the urban planets of [[Denon/Legends|Denon]] and [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].

Her airspeeder had a printed registration sticker as a mining permit for the [[Teraab sector/Legends|Teraab sector]]. The cockpit also contained special oils for the [[Clawdite/Legends|Clawdite's]] use. The left mandible held a secret compartment for her sniper rifle. Additionally, the vehicle contained room in a compartment for her [[ASN-121/Legends|ASN-121]] [[Assassin droid/Legends|assassin droid]].

After her attempt to assassinate [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]] on Coruscant failed, Zam fled from the [[Senator/Legends|Senator's]] [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguards]] in her waiting airspeeder. Zam led the Jedi on a wild chase through [[Galactic City/Legends|Galactic City]], though [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] managed to jump onto her speeder, and in the struggle that followed, Zam accidentally damaged the controls. The ''Koro-2'' spun out of control, crashing into [[Vos Gesal Street/Legends|Vos Gesal Street]] in the [[Uscru Entertainment District/Legends|Uscru District]]. Zam left the flaming wreckage behind, seeking refuge in the [[Outlander Club/Legends|Outlander Club]]. She was killed shortly thereafter by [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]].

''Koro''-2 airspeeders were still in use as of [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]], on worlds such as Coruscant.
